data.title: Buy Rating on a2 Milk Driven by Innovation Pipeline and Chinese Infant Formula Market Tailwinds
data.description:
Analyst Julia de Sterke from Morgan Stanley maintained a Buy rating on a2 Milk Company and keeping the price target at A$10.00.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Julia de Sterke has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including a2 Milk’s strong innovation pipeline and solid medium‑term growth outlook. New product ranges such as Genesis, Kids advanced formulations and seniors offerings are contributing materially to normalized revenue growth, and management sees early signs that prior supply bottlenecks in Stage 1 are easing, which should support renewed user acquisition.

In addition, the company is positioned to benefit from favorable dynamics in the Chinese infant milk formula market, where the e‑commerce led segment is expected to gain share and early‑life formula usage is lengthening as breastfeeding rates decline. a2 Milk targets a meaningful lift in its premium segment share through channel expansion and product upgrades, while a projected recovery in China birth rates from 2026 underpins volume potential; together these support upside versus the current share price and justify a positive recommendation.

In another report released yesterday, Citi also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a A$10.40 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Buy Rating Backed by Occupancy Recovery, Stronger Cash Flows, and Undervalued Yield Opportunity
data.description:
DBS analyst Derek Tan has maintained their bullish stance on OXMU stock, giving a Buy rating today.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Derek Tan has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to improving operations and income visibility. He expects occupancy to rise toward about 90% by FY25/1QFY26, supported by longer leases that extend WALE to 4.7 years, which should boost NPI by roughly 20% and bring earnings close to pre-pandemic levels.

He also highlights management’s decision to lift the payout ratio to 65%, signalling confidence in cash flow recovery, and notes that potential U.S. rate cuts could further lower funding costs and support distributions. With the units trading at roughly 0.4x P/B and offering an attractive forward yield above about 6.6–8.0%, Tan sees significant value, underpinning his DCF-based target price of USD 0.33 and Buy recommendation.

In another report released today, Phillip Securities also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $0.32 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Buy Rating Underpinned by Tightening European Steel Supply and Underappreciated Earnings Upside
data.description:
Analyst Ioannis Masvoulas of Morgan Stanley maintained a Buy rating on ArcelorMittal, with a price target of €46.20.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ioannis Masvoulas has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to tightening steel supply and supportive pricing dynamics in Europe. The reported extended shutdown of the Avilés Blast Furnace B temporarily removes around 1% of regional capacity, but ArcelorMittal can largely compensate lost volumes through other plants, limiting operational disruption at the group level.

At the same time, this outage reinforces the trend toward a structurally undersupplied European steel market under the existing safeguard regime, which helps sustain higher marginal costs and firmer EU HRC price levels. On Masvoulas’s analysis, current spot pricing implies an annualised EBITDA notably above market consensus for 2026, suggesting that earnings expectations remain too conservative and that the stock offers attractive upside potential from its current valuation.

In another report released on February 13, Citi also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a €66.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
